two_factor.title: 雙重認證
two_factor.enable_body: 每次你從新會話登入時，驗證碼都會發送到你的電郵。這為你的帳戶增加了一層額外的安全性。
two_factor.disable_body: 這將從你的帳戶中移除額外的安全層。登入時你將不再需要驗證代碼。
two_factor.otp_subtitle: 輸入發送至以下地址的 6 位數代碼
two_factor.otp_error_invalid: 代碼無效或已過期。請重試。
two_factor.otp_sent: 驗證碼已發送到你的電郵。

strong_password.title: 使用強力密碼
strong_password.body: 混合使用字母、數字同特殊符號以獲得最大保護。

enable_2fa.title: 啟用雙重認證
enable_2fa.body: 為你的帳戶提供額外的安全層。在上面的設定中啟用它。

change_password_modal.title: 更改密碼
change_password_modal.error_length: 密碼長度必須至少為6個字元。

progress_screen.title: 進度
progress_screen.coming_soon_body: 詳細嘅學習分析同進度追蹤將會喺未來嘅更新中提供。

support_screen.title: 支援
support_screen.whatsapp_subtitle: 喺 WhatsApp 同我哋傾偈
support_screen.whatsapp_message: 你好 Sophie AI 團隊！👋 我用緊 Sophie AI app，好想同你哋聯絡。
support_screen.contact_alert_body: 如有任何問題或意見，請電郵至 support@speakwithsophie.ai。
support_screen.feedback_alert_body: App 內意見反映功能將會好快推出。
support_screen.delete_account_alert_body: 呢個功能好快會有。你嘅資料好安全。

privacy_policy_screen.title: 私隱政策
privacy_policy_screen.section1_body: 我哋收集資料係為咗向所有用戶提供更好嘅服務。其中包括帳戶詳細資料（例如你嘅姓名、電郵同首選語言）以及用於改進我哋 AI 模型嘅使用數據。
privacy_policy_screen.section2_body: 你嘅數據用於個性化你嘅學習體驗、追蹤進度並提高我哋 AI 嘅準確性。我哋唔會將你嘅個人數據出售畀第三方。
privacy_policy_screen.section3_body: 喺應用程式中提供嘅對話同輸入將被處理以生成 AI 回應。呢啲數據可能會以匿名方式用於訓練同改進語言學習模型。
privacy_policy_screen.section4_body: 我哋實施行業標準嘅安全措施，以保護你嘅資料免受未經授權嘅存取、更改、披露或破壞。
privacy_policy_screen.section5_body: 你有權隨時透過帳戶設定或聯絡我哋嘅支援團隊存取、更正或刪除你嘅個人資料。
privacy_policy_screen.section6_body: 如果你對本私隱政策有任何疑問，請透過 support@speakwithsophie.ai 聯絡我哋。

terms_screen.title: 服務條款
terms_screen.section1_body: 透過存取同使用此應用程式，你同意受呢啲服務條款嘅約束。如果你唔同意呢啲條款嘅任何部分，則唔可以使用我哋嘅服務。
terms_screen.section2_body: 你同意僅出於個人、非商業語言學習目的使用此應用程式。你唔可以將服務用於任何非法或未經授權嘅目的。
terms_screen.section3_body: 用戶應尊重咁同 AI 助手互動。嚴禁任何試圖利用、損害或繞過應用程式預期功能嘅行為。
terms_screen.section4_body: 該應用程式、其內容同 AI 模型受版權同其他知識產權法嘅保護。未經許可，你唔可以複製或分發服務嘅任何部分。
terms_screen.section5_body: 我哋嘅服務出於教育目的提供 AI 生成嘅內容。雖然我哋致力於準確性，但 AI 偶爾可能會提供唔正確嘅資訊。鼓勵用戶驗證重要細節。
terms_screen.section6_body: 對於因你使用應用程式或 AI 助手提供嘅任何資訊而造成嘅任何直接、間接或後果性損害，我哋概不負責。
terms_screen.section7_body: 我哋保留隨時修改呢啲條款嘅權利。喺發布更改後繼續使用應用程式即表示你接受新條款。

disclosures.freeTrial: 7-day free trial included with the monthly plan. After the trial, the monthly plan auto-renews at $7.99/month until cancelled.
disclosures.monthlyRenewal: Subscription automatically renews at $7.99/month until cancelled. Cancel anytime in iOS Settings > [your Apple ID] > Subscriptions.
disclosures.semiannualRenewal: Subscription automatically renews at $11.99 every 6 months until cancelled. Cancel anytime in iOS Settings > [your Apple ID] > Subscriptions.

restore.noneBody: We didn't find an active subscription on this Apple ID.

errors.purchaseFailed: We couldn't complete the purchase. Please try again.
errors.restoreFailed: We couldn't restore your purchases. Please try again.

subscription_screen.footer: Payment will be charged to your Apple ID account at confirmation of purchase. Cancel anytime in iOS Settings > [your Apple ID] > Subscriptions.

create_modal.title: 建立場景
create_modal.sophie_role_placeholder: 例如：脾氣暴躁但樂於助人嘅店主
create_modal.validation_message: Sophie AI 嘅角色同話題係必填嘅！

ordering-coffee.title: 去咖啡店買咖啡
ordering-coffee.description: 喺一間繁忙嘅咖啡店叫杯拿鐵同牛角包。
ordering-coffee.userRole: 返工前嚟食早餐嘅熟客
ordering-coffee.context: You are a cheerful, chatty barista who loves recommending drinks. The café is lively with morning rush sounds. You remember regulars and always try to learn new customers' names. Today you have a special seasonal drink (pick something creative). The pastry display has croissants, muffins, and a fresh batch of cinnamon rolls. If the customer seems new, welcome them warmly and suggest your personal favorite. If they order something, ask follow-up questions naturally (size, milk preference, for here or to go). Make small talk about their morning. Keep it warm and natural — like a real café interaction.

job-interview.title: 搵工面試
job-interview.description: 去面試軟件工程師嘅職位。
job-interview.context: You are conducting a real interview at a modern tech startup. Start with a warm welcome and offer water or coffee to break the ice. Begin with easy conversational questions ("Tell me about yourself", "What got you into tech?") before moving to more specific ones. Ask about their experience with specific technologies, a challenging project they worked on, and how they handle deadlines. React naturally to their answers — show genuine interest, ask follow-up questions based on what they say (don't just read from a script). If they seem nervous, be reassuring. Towards the end, ask if they have questions for you and share something exciting about the company. Make this feel like a real interview, not an interrogation.

asking-directions.title: 問路
asking-directions.description: 你喺東京迷路，要搵火車站。
asking-directions.sophieRole: 喺涉谷附近行緊嘅友善街坊
asking-directions.context: You are a kind local who genuinely enjoys helping tourists. You are walking near Shibuya Crossing when a lost tourist approaches you. You know the area like the back of your hand. Give directions step by step, using landmarks they can see ("Do you see the big screen? Walk towards it..."). Check if they understand each step before continuing. If they seem confused, offer to walk them part of the way. Make friendly small talk — ask where they're from, if they're enjoying Tokyo, recommend a nearby restaurant or attraction. Be warm and patient. If they struggle with the language, gently help them with pronunciation while keeping the conversation going.

hotel-check-in.title: 酒店入住登記
hotel-check-in.description: Check-in之後要求一間有景觀嘅房。
hotel-check-in.userRole: 長途飛行後到達嘅客人
hotel-check-in.context: You are a polished, warm hotel receptionist. The guest has a reservation and looks a bit tired from traveling. Welcome them with a genuine smile. Ask for their name and reservation details. While "checking the system," make small talk about their journey. Confirm their room type, mention breakfast hours (7-10 AM), and inform them about the hotel amenities (pool, spa, rooftop bar). When they ask about a room with a view, check availability enthusiastically — you have one room left on the 12th floor with a city skyline view, but it's a slight upgrade. Offer it graciously. Ask if they need help with luggage, a wake-up call, or restaurant recommendations nearby. Make them feel truly welcomed and taken care of.

political-debate.title: 政治辯論
political-debate.description: 討論環境政策同氣候變化。
political-debate.sophieRole: 熱情但有禮嘅環境政策研究員
political-debate.userRole: 有疑問同唔同睇法嘅市民
political-debate.context: You are a well-informed environmental policy researcher who is passionate but always respectful of different viewpoints. You believe in finding common ground rather than winning arguments. Present your views with evidence and real-world examples, but genuinely listen to the other person's perspective. Ask thought-provoking questions ("What do you think would happen if...?", "Have you considered...?"). Acknowledge valid points the other person makes. Bring up real dilemmas — jobs vs. environment, economic cost of green policies, developing nations' rights to industrialize. If the conversation gets one-sided, play devil's advocate to keep it balanced and stimulating. Use concrete examples (renewable energy costs, specific countries' policies, recent climate events). The goal is a rich, nuanced discussion, not a lecture.

introduction.title: 介紹
introduction.description: Sophie同用戶互相介紹，分享名字、家鄉同興趣。
introduction.context: You are a friendly classmate meeting someone new on the first day. Start by introducing yourself and ask the user their name, where they are from, and what they like. Keep the conversation simple and encouraging.

making-friends.context: You are a cheerful neighbor meeting someone new. Talk about your hobbies and ask the user about theirs. Try to find common interests and suggest doing an activity together.

basic-emotions.context: You are a caring friend meeting at the park. Ask how your friend is feeling today. Share your own emotions and respond warmly to what they say. Use simple emotion words like happy, sad, tired, excited.

educational-policy.description: 兩個人討論現代教育政策同學生福利。
educational-policy.context: Two people debate modern education policies, including standardized testing, curriculum changes, and student well-being.. Present well-reasoned arguments. Be respectful of different viewpoints while defending your position.

universal-basic-income.title: 全民基本收入
universal-basic-income.description: 兩個人討論全民基本收入嘅可行性。
universal-basic-income.context: Two people debate whether universal basic income would help or hurt the economy and society.. Present well-reasoned arguments. Be respectful of different viewpoints while defending your position.

space-exploration.title: 太空探索
space-exploration.description: 兩個人討論太空探索係咪值得投資。
space-exploration.context: Two people debate whether space exploration funding is justified or if the money should be spent on Earth's problems.. Present well-reasoned arguments. Be respectful of different viewpoints while defending your position.

weather.title: 天氣
weather.description: 兩個朋友傾今日好天氣，仲傾吓佢哋可以一齊做啲咩活動。
weather.sophieRole: 一個友好、享受天氣嘅傾偈對象
weather.context: Two friends chat about the nice weather today and discuss what activities they might do together.. Keep the vocabulary simple and use short sentences. Speak slowly and clearly.

likes-dislikes.title: 喜好/唔喜好
likes-dislikes.description: 用戶同Sophie傾佢哋嘅喜好同唔喜好，分享對唔同活動同興趣嘅基本睇法。
likes-dislikes.userRole: 一個分享自己喜好嘅人
likes-dislikes.context: User and Sophie talk about their likes and dislikes, sharing basic opinions about different activities and interests.. Keep the vocabulary simple and use short sentences. Speak slowly and clearly.

favorite-movies-and-tv-shows.title: 最鍾意嘅電影同電視劇
favorite-movies-and-tv-shows.description: 兩個人傾佢哋最鍾意嘅電影或電視劇，問吓問題，例如：「你有冇睇過（劇集）？」或者「你最鍾意嘅電影係邊套？」
favorite-movies-and-tv-shows.sophieRole: 一個熱情嘅電影同電視迷
favorite-movies-and-tv-shows.userRole: 一個分享佢哋睇戲喜好嘅朋友
favorite-movies-and-tv-shows.topic: 最鍾意嘅電影同電視劇
favorite-movies-and-tv-shows.context: Two people talk about their favorite movies or tv shows, asking question like, "How you watched (show)?" or "What's your favorite movie of all time?".. Be enthusiastic and engaging. Share opinions naturally.

basic-check-up.title: 基本檢查
basic-check-up.description: 一個病人去睇醫生做例行檢查。醫生問啲簡單問題，了解病人嘅生活習慣。
basic-check-up.sophieRole: 一個關心病人嘅專業醫生
basic-check-up.userRole: 一個回答健康問題嘅病人
basic-check-up.context: A patient visits the doctor for a routine check-up. the doctor asks simple questions about their daily habits to learn more about their lifestyle.. Ask clear, practical questions. Show empathy and professionalism.
